title: brightdata-exporter chart values
description: Bright Data observability + FinOps service. Single-replica by design — see https://github.com/danielgines/brightdata-exporter/issues/8 for the architectural background. Bounds for `config.*` mirror the pydantic Settings constraints in src/brightdata_exporter/config.py.

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name component used by the fullname helper.

fullnameOverride.description: Replace the entire computed resource name. Use sparingly — `helm.sh/chart` and ArgoCD tracking IDs assume the default naming convention.

repository.description: Container image without a tag. Default `ghcr.io/danielgines/brightdata-exporter`.

tag.description: Image tag. Empty string falls back to .Chart.AppVersion (the chart-managed pinning).

imagePullSecrets.description: List of Secret references for pulling private images. Each entry is `{name: <secret-name>}`.

replicaCount.description: Single-replica contract. Raising this BREAKS the app: in-process rate limiter, cache, scrape loop, and Prometheus labels all assume one replica. Tracked as architectural work in issue #8 — until that lands, max=1 is enforced by this schema, not just convention.

strategy.description: Deployment update strategy. Default Recreate is correct while replicaCount=1; switch to RollingUpdate after issue #8 with replicas>=2.

auth.description: Bright Data API token plumbing. Set `apiToken` for inline (dev) or `existingSecret` for production (ExternalSecret operator etc.). The exporter will fail to start if neither resolves to a valid token.

existingSecret.description: Name of a pre-created Secret holding the upstream API token. Mutually exclusive with `apiToken`.

existingSecretKey.description: Secret key under `existingSecret` carrying the token. Defaults to BRIGHTDATA_API_TOKEN.

apiToken.description: Inline token value. Convenient for local dev; do NOT commit a real token. Mutually exclusive with `existingSecret`.

apiAuthToken.description: Optional bearer guarding /api/*. Empty leaves /api/* unauthenticated — only safe in trusted networks.

existingAuthSecret.description: Secret holding the /api/* bearer when separate from the API token Secret.

config.description: Exporter configuration — every field is forwarded as a BRIGHTDATA_* environment variable. Bounds mirror pydantic Settings (src/brightdata_exporter/config.py).

apiBase.description: Bright Data API base URL. Override only for testing or corporate proxies.

scrapeInterval.description: Seconds between full scrape cycles. Each cycle issues 1 + N*3 requests (account + per-zone) at 1 req/s; ~45s minimum for 14 zones.

infoCacheSeconds.description: TTL for /zone?zone=NAME results. Plan/usage_limit changes are rare; 1h is safe.

apiRateLimitRps.description: Max requests/s to Bright Data. Default 1.0 matches the documented limit.

zonesFilter.description: Optional regex on zone NAME. Empty = all non-deleted zones.

includeDisabled.description: If true, also scrape cost/bw for status=disabled zones.

collectIpRosters.description: Per-zone /zone/ips + /zone/route_vips. Plan-aware gated; rejected plans return 4xx and are silently skipped.

collectNetworkStatus.description: /network_status/all. Bright Data's own report of the network's operational state.

collectDomainConsumption.description: /domains/bw + /domains/req. High cardinality (one series per zone*domain); off by default.

apiEnabled.description: Mount the on-demand REST endpoints. Set false to run as a metrics-only exporter.

cacheTtlSeconds.description: TTL for cached /api/* responses. 0 disables cache (each request fans out fresh).

cacheMaxSize.description: Max number of cached /api/* keys. LRU eviction past this.

apiMinWindowDays.description: Minimum from/to window enforced on /api/zones*. Bright Data zeroes out billing for sub-day ranges; this clamp expands `from` backward when needed. 0 disables.

logLevel.description: Log verbosity. `warn` is normalized to `warning` at startup.

logFormat.description: Log renderer. `json` for production; `console` for local dev (colors when TTY).

serviceMonitor.description: Prometheus Operator integration. Requires the prometheus-operator CRDs.

relabelings.description: Raw Prometheus Operator RelabelConfig list, copied verbatim.

metricRelabelings.description: Raw RelabelConfig list applied to scraped metrics.

resources.description: Pod resources (requests + limits). Both blocks are recommended — Kyverno require-limits-style policies expect them.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level securityContext. Defaults are PSS Restricted-compatible.

probes.description: Liveness/readiness probes. Both endpoints return 200 while the HTTP server responds — upstream Bright Data health is exposed as the `brightdata_up` metric, not via probe state.

priorityClassName.description: Empty = use cluster default. Set to e.g. `system-cluster-critical` for production preemption protection.

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Window between SIGTERM and SIGKILL. 30s is the k8s default; bump for slow-network clusters where in-flight scrapes need more drain time.

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Default false because the exporter never calls the Kubernetes API. Set true only if a sidecar needs the token.

podDisruptionBudget.description: Default ON, drain-friendly shape (maxUnavailable: 1 + AlwaysAllow). Forward-compatible with issue #8 (replicas>=2) — no spec change needed.

minAvailable.description: Mutually exclusive with `maxUnavailable`. With replicas=1 this BLOCKS drains — anti-pattern, do not use until replicas>=3.

maxUnavailable.description: Default shape. With replicas=1 this is a no-op (drains succeed). With replicas>=2 it caps voluntary disruption at 1 pod.

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: AlwaysAllow (k8s 1.27+, GA 1.31) prevents stuck NotReady/Terminating pods from blocking drains.

autoscaling.description: HPA — COMPLIANCE-ONLY. The app is non-elastic; minReplicas == maxReplicas == 1 is enforced by this schema. Real elasticity is tracked in issue #8.

maxReplicas.description: Pinned at 1 — raising this BREAKS the service (Bright Data 429s, duplicate scrapes, overlapping Prometheus series). See issue #8.

targetCPUUtilizationPercentage.description: CPU utilization target for the synthesized metric. Ignored when `metrics` is non-empty.

metrics.description: Raw HorizontalPodAutoscaler.spec.metrics list. When non-empty, replaces the synthesized CPU metric entirely. Required to be non-empty by HPA v2 — the chart synthesizes a CPU metric when this is empty so the rendered manifest is always valid.

behavior.description: Raw HPA v2 scaleUp/scaleDown behavior block.

networkPolicy.description: Opt-in. Requires a CNI that enforces NetworkPolicy (Calico, Cilium, Antrea, kube-router). flannel/kindnet/AWS VPC CNI without addons admit but do NOT filter — silent breakage.

from.description: Raw NetworkPolicyPeer list. Empty + enabled=true denies all ingress.

extra.description: Raw NetworkPolicyEgressRule list. Use for FQDN egress (Cilium/Calico Enterprise) or corporate proxy access.

probe.description: Standard Kubernetes Probe. Either httpGet, tcpSocket, or exec is required.
